No external data access, network calls, or dynamic code execution are observed.\n\n2. **Obfuscation or malware indicators:**  \n   The only anomaly noted is the presence of a base64-encoded source map comment (`//# sourceMappingURL=...`). This is typical for transpiled or minified code and does not imply obfuscation or malicious intent. No hardcoded secrets, credentials, or suspicious patterns are detected.\n\n3. **Functionality and reasoning flaws:**  \n   The functions are straightforward, mathematically correct, and well-documented. No logical flaws or reasoning errors are evident.\n\n4. **Scores assigned in reports:**  \n   All reports assign a malware score of 0, which aligns with the benign nature of the code.
